title: goose: no logging on nftables drops
description: The nftables input and forward chains use 'policy drop' but have no logging rules before the implicit drop. This makes it difficult to debug why traffic is being dropped. Consider adding 'log prefix "nft-drop: " counter drop' as the final explicit rule in each chain, or at least for specific interface combinations. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix.

title: pakhet: remove /etc/hosts entry for git.unixpimps.net
description:
pakhet has a manual /etc/hosts entry for git.unixpimps.net that works around the broken NAT hairpinning on the wired VLAN. Once dotfiles-skv (hairpinning fix) is deployed, this workaround should be removed.

Look for the hosts entry in pakhet's NixOS configuration (networking.extraHosts or similar) and remove it.

title: pakhet: remove legacy opsplaza module
description:
Remove the old JBoss-based opsplaza module (hosts/pakhet/services/opsplaza.nix) and all related infrastructure now that themailer-wrapper has replaced it.

## Context

The opsplaza service was the original JBoss-based themailer running in podman containers (CouchDB 2.3.1 + JBoss AS 7 + postfix relay). It has been replaced by themailer-wrapper, a Rust/axum rewrite with SQLite that runs as a single systemd service.

The old module is currently disabled (`services.opsplaza.enable = false` in default.nix) but all the code is still present.

## What to remove

1. **`hosts/pakhet/services/opsplaza.nix`** — delete entirely (podman containers, couchdb backup, borgbackup job, nginx vhost, tmpfiles, podman network)
2. **`hosts/pakhet/services/default.nix`** — remove `./opsplaza.nix` from imports, remove `services.opsplaza.enable = false;`
3. **`flake.nix`** — remove the `opsplaza` input (`tarball+https://git.unixpimps.net/ijohanne/opsplaza-artifacts/archive/master.tar.gz`), remove from outputs function parameters
4. **`flake.lock`** — will update automatically after removing the input
5. **`secrets/pakhet.yaml`** — consider removing `opsplaza_smtp_pass` if no longer needed (check: themailer-wrapper reuses it via sops template for SMTP credentials, so it may still be needed under that name)
6. **`scripts/migrate-opsplaza-couchdb.sh`** — delete the migration script
7. **`flake.nix` devShell** — remove `migrate-opsplaza-couchdb` and its `gsed-compat` helper from devShells.default packages
8. **On pakhet server** — clean up `/var/opsplaza/couchdb` data directory and `/var/backup/opsplaza` after confirming no longer needed
9. **Gitea** — archive or delete the `opsplaza-artifacts` repo on git.unixpimps.net

## Verification

- `nix flake check` passes
- pakhet deploys successfully
- `themailer.opsplaza.com` still works via themailer-wrapper
- No podman containers or images remain

comments.author: Ian Johannesen
comments.text:
Additional cleanup items:

1. hosts/pakhet/configuration.nix — remove the nix-netrc sops template (lines 30-37) and the netrc-file line from nix.extraOptions (line 27). These exist solely to authenticate pakhet's nix daemon to git.unixpimps.net for fetching the opsplaza-artifacts tarball. The nix_builder_access_tokens line (26) may still be needed — verify before removing.

2. hosts/pakhet/services/default.nix — remove sops.secrets.gitea_access_token declaration (only used by the netrc template above)

3. secrets/pakhet.yaml — remove gitea_access_token (only needed for opsplaza-artifacts fetching). Keep opsplaza_smtp_pass — it is reused by themailer-wrapper SMTP credentials template (themailer@unixpimps.net password). Consider renaming it to something less opsplaza-specific.

4. secrets/pakhet.yaml — check if nix_builder_access_tokens contains tokens for registries beyond gitea before removing

5. hosts/pakhet/services/default.nix — the duplicate sops.secrets.nix_builder_access_tokens declaration (also in configuration.nix) — verify which is actually needed

title: goose: camera VLAN has lateral access to all internal VLANs
description: The nftables forward chain allows camera VLAN to reach wifi/wired/mgnt and vice versa via the inter-VLAN accept rule. Cameras should typically only reach the UNVR and the internet, not workstations or servers. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, forward chain line 56-57.

title: goose: no 'ct state invalid drop' in nftables ruleset
description: The nftables ruleset has no rule to drop packets with invalid conntrack state. Invalid packets are accepted by the blanket accept rules. Best practice is to add 'ct state invalid drop' early in both the input and forward chains. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix.

title: goose: ppp0 not in flowtable, no flow offload for PPPoE traffic
description: The flowtable 'fastnat' includes wifi, wired, camera, mgnt, and wan but NOT ppp0. Since the primary WAN is PPPoE (ppp0), flow offload does not accelerate the main internet traffic path. Adding ppp0 to the flowtable devices would enable hardware/software offload for PPPoE sessions. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, flowtable lines 65-68.

title: goose: SMTP port 25 open on WAN with no SMTP server
description: The nftables input chain accepts TCP port 25 from 0.0.0.0/0, including from ppp0/WAN. There is no SMTP server configured on goose. This is an unnecessary open port on the public internet. Either remove it or, if it was intended for forwarding, move it to the forward chain with a DNAT rule. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, input chain line 22.

title: goose: IPv6 forwarding enabled but unused
description: net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding is set to true but no IPv6 addresses are configured on any VLAN interface, no IPv6 firewall rules exist, and no IPv6 NAT/masquerade. Either implement IPv6 support or remove the forwarding sysctl to reduce attack surface. File: hosts/goose/networking.nix line 6.

title: goose: nftables forward chain rule ordering is unconventional
description: In the forward chain, the MSS clamp and flow offload rules appear before the 'type filter hook forward priority filter; policy drop;' declaration. While nftables is lenient about this, the type/hook/policy line should conventionally come first for readability. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, forward chain lines 44-46.

title: goose: test_connection script does not actually fail over
description: The test_connection script in configuration.nix claims to test WAN connectivity and switch to backup, but the 'Changing to backup' branch just prints a message and exits 0. It never modifies the routing table despite requiring root and claiming it will. Either implement the failover logic or remove the misleading script. File: hosts/goose/configuration.nix lines 44-76.

title: goose: guest and camera VLANs have full access to router services
description: The nftables input chain accepts ALL traffic from all internal interfaces including guest and camera. The blanket 'iifname { wifi, wired, mgnt, guest, camera, wan, lo, wg0 } counter accept' means guest clients can reach every service on goose (Prometheus 9001, Grafana 2342, SSH 22, unbound-control 8953, all exporters, nginx). Guest should only get DNS (53) + DHCP (67/68) + routing. Camera should only get DNS + DHCP + UNVR access. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, input chain lines 25-34.

title: nftables flow offloading broken on kernel 6.19.5
description:
## Summary

nftables flowtable flow offloading is completely broken on Linux kernel 6.19.5 (NixOS stable 25.11). Any TCP connection that gets offloaded via `flow add @fastnat` stalls during the initial handshake — SSH shows "Connection timed out during banner exchange", TLS handshakes fail. This affects ALL interfaces in the flowtable (wifi, wired, camera, mgnt), not just wan/ppp0.

Flow offloading is currently disabled on goose. This means all inter-VLAN traffic goes through the full nftables chain on every packet, which impacts throughput on the 2x25G (goose↔sw10) and 10G SFP+ links.

## Impact

- **goose ↔ sw10**: 2x25G LACP — inter-VLAN forwarding at high rates will CPU-bottleneck without offloading
- **fatty ↔ sw10**: 2x25G LACP — bulk transfers to/from VMs (pakhet, thoth, horus) affected
- **7 switches with 10G SFP+ uplinks** — all inter-VLAN traffic routes through goose
- 1 Gbps PPPoE WAN uplink is unaffected (CPU can handle 1G without offloading)

## Root Cause

Unknown kernel regression in the nftables flowtable/flow offload path in 6.19.x. The known 6.19.4 regression (pipapo set backend crash, fixed in 6.19.5) is a different bug. This flow offload regression has not been identified in upstream kernel changelogs yet.

## Current State

- `hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix`: flowtable `fastnat` is defined with `devices = { wifi, wired, camera, mgnt }` but no `flow offload @fastnat` rule exists in the forward chain
- Flow offloading is effectively disabled
- PPP ip-up hook restarts unbound, smokeping-exporter, and cloudflare-dyndns on reconnection (networking.nix)

## How to Test if a New Kernel Fixes This

### 1. Check the current kernel on goose
```bash
ssh 10.255.254.254 "uname -r"
```

### 2. Add a LAN-to-LAN scoped flow offload rule (live, no deploy needed)
```bash
ssh 10.255.254.254 'echo "insert rule ip filter forward position 26 iifname { wifi, wired, camera, mgnt } oifname { wifi, wired, camera, mgnt } ip protocol { tcp, udp } flow add @fastnat" | sudo nft -f -'
```
Note: position 26 is after the LAN→WAN accept rule. Get the correct handle with:
```bash
ssh 10.255.254.254 'sudo nft -a list chain ip filter forward'
```
Insert the flow offload rule right before the LAN-to-LAN accept rule (the one with `iifname { wg0, wan, wifi, mgnt, wired, camera } oifname { ... } counter accept`).

### 3. Test SSH through the forwarding path
```bash
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 10.255.101.200 echo ok
```
If this returns "ok" — flow offloading works. If it times out with "banner exchange" — still broken.

### 4. Clean up (remove the test rule if broken)
```bash
ssh 10.255.254.254 'handle=$(sudo nft -a list chain ip filter forward | grep "flow add" | awk "{print \$NF}"); sudo nft delete rule ip filter forward handle $handle'
```

### 5. If fixed, re-enable permanently
Add back to `hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix` in the forward chain, before the LAN-to-LAN accept rule:
```nix
iifname { "wifi", "wired", "camera", "mgnt" } oifname {
  "wifi", "wired", "camera", "mgnt" } ip protocol { tcp, udp } flow offload @fastnat
```
Do NOT use a blanket `ip protocol { tcp, udp } flow offload @fastnat` — this would offload PPPoE traffic and bypass MSS clamping (tcp option maxseg size set 1452), causing PMTU blackhole issues.

## How to Check for New Kernels in nixpkgs-stable

```bash
# Check what kernel NixOS stable (25.11) currently provides:
nix eval nixpkgs-stable#linuxPackages.kernel.version

# Or check the latest available:
nix eval nixpkgs-stable#linuxPackages_latest.kernel.version
```

## Related Commits

- 827944f — Fix PPPoE flow offloading and boot ordering on goose (initial attempt)
- 774b82a — Disable flow offloading entirely on goose (kernel 6.19.5 regression)

title: goose: NAT hairpinning broken for wired VLAN (same-subnet as pakhet)
description:
NAT hairpinning fails when a client on the wired VLAN (10.255.101.0/24) tries to access services via the WAN IP. It works fine from other VLANs (wifi, etc).

**Root cause:** Missing masquerade for same-subnet hairpinned traffic in `hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix`.

When a wired client (10.255.101.x) hits goose's WAN IP on port 443, goose DNATs to pakhet (10.255.101.200). But postrouting line 86 has no action:
```
oifname { "wifi", "wired", "camera", "mgnt" }
```
So the packet arrives at pakhet with the original client source IP. Since pakhet and the client are on the same L2 segment, pakhet responds directly to the client, bypassing goose. The client drops the response because the source IP (10.255.101.200) doesn't match the WAN IP it connected to.

**Fix:** Add masquerade for DNAT'd traffic re-entering the wired VLAN:
```
oifname "wired" ip daddr 10.255.101.200 ct status dnat masquerade
```
This ensures pakhet sees goose's wired IP (10.255.101.254) as source, routes the reply back through goose, and goose un-DNATs + un-SNATs correctly.

**File:** `hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix` line 86
notes:
**Testing:** Verify that the fix can be generalized beyond just pakhet. Instead of hardcoding `ip daddr 10.255.101.200`, consider using `ct status dnat masquerade` for any DNAT'd traffic re-entering the same VLAN. This covers cctax-node (10.255.101.245) and any future services added to the wired VLAN without needing additional firewall rules. E.g.:

```
iifname "wired" oifname "wired" ct status dnat masquerade
```

title: goose: ICMP accepted from all sources without rate limiting
description: The nftables input chain accepts all ICMP from everywhere including WAN with no rate limit ('ip protocol icmp accept'). Should at minimum rate-limit ICMP from WAN interfaces to prevent ICMP flood attacks. File: hosts/goose/services/firewall.nix, input chain line 23.
